top_deep_findings: Topology is sound as an index over authority, not as authority itself; the repo states this correctly, but some tooling behavior still acts as if every global drift issue is a local blocker.
Navigation still aggregates too many global repo-health lanes; it can fail because docs/source/history/reference drift exists even when route context is usable.
Closeout has partial changed-file scoping, but companion expansion and blocking semantics are still lane-by-lane heuristics rather than a typed issue policy.
TopologyIssue is too thin for machine routing: code/path/message/severity is not enough to decide ownership, scope, repair kind, blocking modes, related paths, or maturity.
The machine layer is denser than the cognition layer: manifests and tests contain far more operational knowledge than module books and scoped AGENTS surfaces.
Module books exist, but the topology and graph books are explicit about being too thin; several system books needed by online-only agents are missing or underdeveloped.

Test topology and tests encode major governance laws that are not promoted into durable reference books, including archive interface rules, graph protocol behavior, compiled-topology shape, and current-state receipt bindings.
Module manifest is a valuable router but still skeletal/placeholder in policy fields, and it risks duplicating docs/source/test/script ownership unless normalized.

Graph integration is directionally correct as derived context, warning-first and semantic-boot-after; its online value remains limited because the tracked graph.db is binary and needs textual extracts.
The topology system is repair-hostile: many failures identify drift but do not emit repair blueprint metadata, proposed stubs, or owner-manifest-driven next actions.
top_structural_changes: Introduce lane policy and typed issues before adding more manifest rows.
Rehydrate dense system/module books for topology, docs, manifests, topology_doctor, graph, and closeout/receipts.
Normalize ownership so each manifest owns one kind of truth and topology compiles rather than duplicates it.
Add graph/context-pack textual extraction as advisory derived context, never authority.
Split deterministic topology_doctor tests from live repo-health tests and encode hidden laws in durable references.

navigation.failure_mode: Aggregates docs/source/history/reference health; error severity blocks route
navigation.desired_role: Generate usable route digest; direct blockers only
navigation.blocking_policy: Block only unreadable required authority, no route, requested-file obligation failure
navigation.missing_metadata: lane, scope, blocking_modes, requested_paths, owner_manifest, repair_kind

closeout.failure_mode: Partial scoping; always-on lanes can still overblock; companion expansion weak
closeout.desired_role: Changed-file + required-companion gate with global health sidecar
closeout.blocking_policy: Block scoped issues and undeferred required companions
closeout.improvements: Use typed issue policy and companion graph

strict.failure_mode: Useful but can be conflated with navigation
strict.blocking_policy: Block all severity=error and selected warnings if promoted

docs.failure_mode: Docs drift can block unrelated topology navigation
docs.desired_role: Docs system health and scoped docs closeout
docs.blocking_policy: Block docs packet or strict; warn in unrelated navigation

source.failure_mode: Missing rationale appears global; source knowledge thin
source.blocking_policy: Block changed source; strict full source audit

tests.failure_mode: Live repo classifications and fixture laws mixed
tests.blocking_policy: Block test changes and strict; fixture tests deterministic

scripts.failure_mode: Good safety metadata but huge manifest; failures not repair-grouped
scripts.blocking_policy: Block changed scripts and strict; warn unrelated navigation

graph.failure_mode: Binary graph under-realized; stale/missing can confuse agents
graph.blocking_policy: Never semantic blocker unless task explicitly requires graph evidence

reference replacement.failure_mode: Hidden until docs/reference work; can block broadly
reference replacement.desired_role: Prevent stale references and removed support doc leaks

history lore.failure_mode: Lore can become default encyclopedia or noisy drift
history lore.desired_role: Extract durable lessons; keep lore non-default
history lore.blocking_policy: Block only declared fatal-misread/lore schema gates
history lore.improvements: Promote recurring rules to module books

context packs.failure_mode: Can inherit manifest thinness and graph unreadability
context packs.desired_role: Route-aware context with limitations and module cognition
context packs.blocking_policy: Block only when route health for requested input is unusable
context packs.improvements: Use module books and graph text sidecars

compiled topology.failure_mode: Shape encoded in tests more than docs
compiled topology.blocking_policy: Block strict/compiled lane when schema broken

repair drafts.blocking_policy: Never auto-block except missing draft generator in its packet

topology.yaml.should_own: Root/zone/path coverage, top-level docs subroots, active pointer requirements, digest/core-map profiles, derived compiled topology inputs.
topology.yaml.should_not_own: Per-file source rationale, test categories, script lifecycle, module book content, docs truth details.
topology.yaml.drift_risk: Duplicates docs_registry and module_manifest if it tries to catalog every file; stale coverage roots can overroute.

docs_registry.yaml.should_own: Docs classification: doc_class, default_read, truth_profile, freshness_class, lifecycle, direct-reference behavior, parent coverage.
docs_registry.yaml.should_not_own: Module ownership, source write routes, test law gates, script safety, current packet status beyond classification.
docs_registry.yaml.drift_risk: Can become huge and duplicative with docs AGENTS and topology docs_subroots.

module_manifest.yaml.should_own: Module-to-book/scoped-AGENTS router; module hazards; high-risk files; law/current-fact/test links at module level; graph appendix status.
module_manifest.yaml.should_not_own: File-level source rationale, docs classification, script lifecycle, test categories.
module_manifest.yaml.drift_risk: Risks duplicating source_rationale/test_topology/docs_registry; placeholder fields can look authoritative before being mature.

source_rationale.yaml.should_own: Tracked src file rationale, zone, authority role, hazards, write routes, package defaults, source/scoped AGENTS coherence.
source_rationale.yaml.should_not_own: Module book prose, test classification, script metadata, docs classification.
source_rationale.yaml.drift_risk: May become an exhaustive source encyclopedia; missing upstream/downstream makes hidden routes.

test_topology.yaml.should_own: Test category, law-gate tests/protects, high-sensitivity skips, reverse-antibody status, relationship test manifests.
test_topology.yaml.should_not_own: General module ownership, docs registry, source write routes.
test_topology.yaml.drift_risk: Law gates can carry hidden architecture law without reference extraction.

script_manifest.yaml.should_own: Top-level script lifecycle/class/safety/write targets/apply flags/target DB/required tests.
script_manifest.yaml.should_not_own: Source code behavior, module books, docs classification.
script_manifest.yaml.drift_risk: Huge manifest can hide operational facts; script helper centrality not obvious.

map_maintenance.yaml.should_own: Companion update rules for added/deleted/renamed/modified surfaces; narrow registry ownership gate.
map_maintenance.yaml.should_not_own: Broad prose rewrite obligations or global health.
map_maintenance.yaml.drift_risk: Can overblock if mode/scoping not typed; required companions need repair hints.

context_budget.yaml.should_own: Default-read/budget posture, thin routers vs dense module books, advisory context bloat warnings.
context_budget.yaml.should_not_own: Suppression of required authority reads or content classification.
context_budget.yaml.drift_risk: Can be mistaken for hard read limits; should remain advisory unless promoted.

artifact_lifecycle.yaml.should_own: Artifact classes, liminal artifact roles, work-record contract, evidence vs authority behavior.
artifact_lifecycle.yaml.should_not_own: Docs registry classifications and packet status details.
artifact_lifecycle.yaml.drift_risk: Can overlap with docs_registry/artifacts AGENTS; closeout integration must be scoped.

code_review_graph_protocol.yaml.should_own: Graph use protocol, semantic-boot-before-graph, forbidden uses, verification gates, official graph commands.
code_review_graph_protocol.yaml.should_not_own: Graph-derived findings, source truth, authority rank, settlement/source correctness.
code_review_graph_protocol.yaml.drift_risk: Graph facts can be overtrusted unless output remains labeled derived.

history_lore.yaml.should_own: Failure modes, wrong moves, antibodies, residual risks, task routing lessons.
history_lore.yaml.should_not_own: Active law that should be in authority, module book explanations that should be default discoverable.
history_lore.yaml.drift_risk: Lore can become a noisy encyclopedia if not extracted into module books.

invariants.yaml.should_not_own: Implementation-specific route advice or current packet state.
invariants.yaml.drift_risk: Tests may enforce invariant meanings more concretely than docs.

negative_constraints.yaml.should_not_own: Operational current state or packet exceptions.
negative_constraints.yaml.drift_risk: May need mapping from negative constraint to repair route.

blockers: Local test execution was not performed in this environment.
Tracked graph.db was confirmed as present from GitHub listing but not directly inspected as authority.
Repo may move after this package; reproduce locally at the supplied baseline commit.
do_not_do: mix runtime/source behavior changes with topology reform
